title: Dehydrated Smoky German Pork Skewers
description: A smoky and crispy German dish featuring pickle juice, perfect for a snack or light meal. The dehydrating process gives the pork a tender and energizing texture.
instructions: Preheat the dehydrator to 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together pickle juice, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, black pepper, and brown sugar.
Add the pork pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the marinade.
Thread the marinated pork pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers in the dehydrator and dehydrate for 6 hours.
After 6 hours, increase the dehydrator temperature to 200 degrees Fahrenheit and dehydrate for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the skewers from the dehydrator and let them cool completely on a wire rack.
Serve the smoky German pork skewers warm or at room temperature.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
